mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at or near the zero lower bound, equity returns over the next 20 trading days are positive if CPIAUCSL is stable or easing because real-rate pressure stays contained and discount-rate support dominates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if inflation acceleration does not weaken the signal at all over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand; when unemployment increases from the prior month, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative as earnings expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus the prior monthly reading
failure_condition: Reject if monthly unemployment increases are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or if rank correlation with forward returns stays above zero for 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Strengthening RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending and revenue growth; when retail sales accelerate from the prior month, S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are positive because near-term earnings revisions improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth turns positive and exceeds its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive retail sales momentum is not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with firm PAYEMS indicates broad production and hiring momentum; when both improve simultaneously, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive because cyclical earnings and risk appetite rise together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample hit rate, or if joint-positive months fail to deliver positive 20d returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST flags housing demand weakness and a future slowdown in consumption and credit-sensitive sectors; when housing starts fall materially, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if large housing-start declines are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of episodes, or if the signal does not remain negative after excluding recession months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real-rate compression is likely to persist and equities should advance over the next 20 trading days as policy stays accommodative relative to prices.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above the prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation accelerations under low policy rates fail to produce positive 20d returns, or if the sign flips after controlling for broad market trend in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll contraction and weaker aggregate income; when employment growth turns negative, S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are negative because the market reprices near-term growth risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month over month for two consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the relationship is not visible in any post-2009 subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad final-demand acceleration; when both trend higher, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are positive because earnings breadth and sales momentum support valuation expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales strength fails to improve 60d forward returns relative to either proxy alone, or if joint-up periods do not beat the unconditional benchmark over any 4-year rolling window.
